exemplar.title:
To what extent does the variation in the throat area of a
nozzle affect both the maximum thrust and the duration of a sugar-fueled
rocket motor?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent does the variation in the throat area of a
nozzle affect both the maximum thrust and the duration of a sugar-fueled
rocket motor?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question is clearly stated, narrow, and consistently referenced throughout the report.
Terminology and signposting (headings, table of contents) make the topic readily understandable to unfamiliar readers.
Methodology is complete: apparatus, variables, procedure, safety and data collection are well documented.
weaknesses: Minor ambiguity between âspecific impulseâ in the RQ/title and the variables actually measured (thrust and burn time).
A few editorial lapses (typos, duplicate section numbers) slightly detract from precision.
feedback.calculationMethod:
Holistic AI evaluation: The work communicates its topic and focus effectively and formulates a clear, focused research question with strong connection to the discussion. The methodology is mostly complete with appropriate range of methods and clear evidence of informed selection, but slight inconsistencies (specific impulse vs.
thrust/burn time) and editorial errors prevent a topâband 6. A bandâ5 mark (out of 6) best reflects these strengths and remaining imprecision.

strengths: Accurate derivation and notation of the thrust equation and massâflow rate concepts.
Clear presentation of the candy rocket fuel chemical reaction in LaTeX, aiding precise communication.
Use of relevant physics terminology (massâflow rate, choked flow) and a schematic that supports conceptual understanding.
weaknesses: Conceptual slips: treating thrustâarea relationship without chamber pressure considerations and inconsistent statements on burn time proportionality.
Specific impulse is never calculated despite being central to the stated aim.
Minor notation and symbol inconsistencies (units, gas constant symbol) and typographical errors in definitions.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report demonstrates good subject knowledge with mostly relevant source application and coherent understanding, but several conceptual inaccuracies and omissions (notably the missing specific impulse calculation) prevent it from reaching the highest band. A midâband score of 4 (out of 6) accurately reflects the workâs strengths and gaps.

strengths: Data analysis includes best/worstâfit lines, RÂ² values, and justified exclusion of an outlier.
Argument development is logical and links theoretical expectations to observed trends, with a coherent structure (IntroâMethodâResultsâConclusionâEvaluation).
Evaluation section identifies key limitations (sampling rate, fuel humidity) and proposes targeted improvements (millisecond logging, fuel drying).
weaknesses: Analysis is based on only five data points and ignores uncertainty propagation or sensor bias in negative thrust readings.
Assumptions underlying the thrust equation (isentropic flow, adiabatic conditions) are not discussed.
Evaluation is relatively brief and does not quantify the impact of limitations or address theoretical model validity in depth.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The critical thinking is solidly in the 7â9 band: analysis and argument development are wellâfocused on the research question, and there is meaningful critical evaluation of strengths and weaknesses. However, the work lacks the depth of uncertainty analysis and continuous, integrated critique required for the top band. An 8 (out of 12) reflects its overall strong but not exemplary performance.

strengths: Clear title page, table of contents, sectional headings and page numbering support navigation.
Figures and tables are included and generally labelled, aiding comprehension.
weaknesses: Formatting lapses: cross-reference errors, duplicated section numbers, raw code blocks disrupt flow.
Several low-resolution images and overlapping axis labels detract from visual clarity.
Figure captions and source details are sometimes missing or misplaced.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets basic structural and formatting conventions but contains multiple lapses that reduce polish and readability. These features align with band 1â2; a score of 2 (out of 4) accurately reflects the generally appropriate structure with some missing elements.

strandSelections.reasoning: Physics terms such as âmass-flow rateâ, âspecific heat ratioâ, âchoked flowâ and Arduino sensor terminology are used generally correctly. However, spelling/grammatical errors, repeated misuse of symbols (e.g., units on axes, missing Ã10â»Â³ labels) and the confusion between specific and total impulse show minor inaccuracies. Consistency is reasonable but not flawless, situating the work in the lower half of band 3-4; a score of 3 reflects this.

The narrative links theoretical expectations to observed positive/negative correlations and discusses an anomaly at 15.39 Ã 10â»âµ mÂ². The conclusion revisits the RQ and synthesises findings. Structure is logical (Intro-Method-Results-Conclusion-Evaluation). Reasoning is mostly coherent, though some assertions (direct proportionality) lack deeper justification and the argument would benefit from more rigorous statistical treatment. These features place the work solidly in the âwell-developedâ 4-6 range; mark 5 is appropriate.

**Evidence of completeness** â The student details apparatus, variables, controlled variables, calibration code, procedure and safety. Multiple throat diameters are prepared and five usable datasets collected. Relevant background equations are supplied and linked to the chosen measurements.  
**Limitations** â Methodological rigour is moderate: only one independent variable level discarded, limited repetition (three trials per diameter), large 1 s time intervals, negative thrust artefacts remain unexplained, and no calculation of the promised specific impulse. Source selection is largely popular/NASA web pages; peer-reviewed physics literature is scant.  
The methodology is therefore âmostly complete with generally relevant methods and some evidence of informed selectionâ, matching band 3-4; the stronger end (4) is selected.

**Positive indicators** â Section 2 discusses mass-flow rate, de Laval nozzles, thrust equation and uses relevant variables (Î³, R, Pt). The student applies these ideas to justify data trends.  
**Deficiencies** â Conceptual slips appear: thrust is treated as directly proportional to throat area without considering chamber pressure changes; âburning time is directly proportionalâ is mis-stated as inverse earlier; specific impulse is never calculated; negative thrust readings are accepted without explanation of sensor bias.  
Overall knowledge is âgoodâ rather than excellent: appropriate but not consistently rigorous, warranting a band 3-4 score of 4.

The Evaluation section reflects on strengths (custom load-cell rig, washers) and weaknesses (1 s sampling, humid propellant) and proposes improvements (millisecond logging, drying fuel). However, evaluation is relatively brief, does not quantify the impact of limitations, and omits consideration of systematic sensor errors or theoretical model validity. This demonstrates âsome critical analysisâ but not integrated, continuous critique; thus lower end of band 4-6, score 4.

**Strengths** â The research question, purpose and context are introduced in the first paragraphs and repeated in the contents page. Terminology such as âthroat areaâ, âspecific impulseâ and âsugar-fuelled rocket motorâ signal a clear, physics-based focus. Headings, a table of contents and signposting make the topic readily understandable to readers unfamiliar with the work.  
**Weaknesses** â Minor ambiguity arises because the RQ alternates between âspecific impulseâ (title/introduction) and âmaximum thrust + burn timeâ (contents/results), creating slight lack of precision. A few editorial lapses (âdireclyâ, duplicate section numbers) detract from an otherwise clear communication.  
Given the overall clarity and explicit focus, but with some inconsistencies, the strand aligns most closely with the upper band (5-6), but not the very top; a score of 5 is appropriate.

Figures, tables and graphs are included and labelled, but several images are low resolution, axis labels overlap, and unit multipliers (Ã10â»Â³) are inconsistent. The overall layout supports comprehension yet exhibits notable visual inconsistencies, placing it in the basic but acceptable 1-2 band; score 2.

**Strengths** â The RQ is explicitly stated, narrow (variation of nozzle throat area on thrust and burn time) and consistently referenced in Background, Variables, Results, Conclusion and Evaluation. The essay maintains focus on this single engineering parameter throughout.  
**Weaknesses** â Occasional drift occurs when the introduction frames the aim as finding âspecific impulseâ yet the collected data never calculate this variable, indicating a slight mismatch between RQ and execution.  
Despite this, the RQ is still clear and mostly well-focused, so the strand best fits band 5-6; a mid-band mark of 5 is justified.

**Analysis** â Raw data are manipulated to produce thrust, burn time and percentage errors; graphs with best/worst-fit lines and RÂ² values are provided; an outlier is identified and excluded with justification.  
**Limits** â Analysis rests mainly on simple linear correlations derived from only five data points; no propagation of uncertainties into gradient/intercept; negative thrust values are ignored rather than interpreted.  
Thus, analysis is âgoodâ and clearly related to the RQ, but not âexcellentâ or exhaustive, aligning with mid-band 4-6; score 5.

A clear title page, contents table, sectional headings and page numbers are present, but there are formatting lapses: erroneous cross-reference (âError! Bookmark not definedâ), duplicated section numbering (2.6 twice), figure captions occasionally misplaced, and raw code blocks break flow. These issues indicate âgenerally appropriate structure with some missing elementsâ, corresponding to band 1-2; the better value 2 is awarded.

annotations.comment: Weakness: The sentence âThe mass of any object is simply the volume that the object occupies the density of the objectâ is unclear and likely a typo. Clarify the relationship between mass, density and volume.
Strength: The student correctly includes a word count, satisfying the formatting requirement and demonstrating attention to administrative details.
Suggestion: The research question mentions âspecific impulseâ but the data focus on thrust and burn time. Align the RQ wording with the variables actually measured to improve precision.
Weakness: The table of contents shows âError! Bookmark not defined.â and duplicated section numbers (â2.6â twice), which disrupts navigation and should be corrected.

Weakness: The introduction frames the aim as finding specific impulse, yet no calculation of specific impulse appears in the methodology or results. Consider computing this variable to fulfill the stated aim.

Weakness: The glossary defines specific impulse simply as âchange in momentum per unit massâ but omits the standard gravity term. Refine this definition to reflect the conventional $$I_{sp} = \frac{F}{\dot m g_0}$$.
Strength: Inclusion of a schematic (Figure 2.1) helps visualize the thrust generation process. To strengthen academic rigor, reference a primary source rather than a popular education site.
Weakness: Several images on this page are low resolution and axis labels overlap. Improve image quality and adjust label placement to support clear communication.
Suggestion: Heading â2.3 Model Rocketsâ uses an extra bold tag compared to other headings. Standardize heading formatting for consistency.
Strength: The chemical reaction for candy rocket fuel is presented clearly in LaTeX, aiding precise communication of stoichiometry.
Strength: The derivation $$m = \rho A v t$$ and $$\dot m = \rho A v$$ is correctly presented, showing clear understanding of mass flow rate.
Suggestion: In the relation $$\dot m = \rho A v$$, explicitly state units for each variable (e.g., kg/s) to reinforce dimensional understanding.
Weakness: The notation for the specific gas constant is shown as â\hat{R}â in the list. Use the standard symbol $$R$$ or $$\overline{R}$$ to avoid confusion.
Weakness: Typographical error âdireclyâ instead of âdirectlyâ undermines professionalism. Correct spelling throughout the report.
Strength: The combined thrust equation $$\sum F = \dot m v_e + (p_e - p_a)A_e$$ is accurately derived and clearly notated, indicating solid conceptual understanding.
Suggestion: Before presenting the thrust equation, discuss key assumptions (e.g., adiabatic and isentropic flow) to ground the derivation in physical context.
Weakness: The description of the de Laval nozzle omits design criteria such as optimal pressure ratio. Expand on how throat and exit areas are chosen for peak performance.
Suggestion: The figure caption for the convergent-divergent nozzle lacks a page reference. Include precise source details for credibility.
Suggestion: The dependent variables list omits specific impulse, which is central to the RQ. Either include its calculation or adjust the DV description.
Suggestion: Consider controlling ambient temperature and pressure as additional variables, since they affect gas properties and thus thrust measurements.
Weakness: The apparatus list displays inconsistent bullet symbols and items like âCat litterâ lack technical description. Standardize the list and clarify each componentâs role.
Weakness: The description of washer use for nozzle areas lacks detail on assembly tolerances. Explain how consistent throat diameters were achieved experimentally.
Weakness: The calibration table in Figure 4.7 contains typos (âqramsâ) and inconsistent formatting. Clean up labels and verify units for clarity.
Suggestion: Graphs in the results (e.g., Figure 6.1) lack error bars on thrust and burn time. Including uncertainty bars would strengthen the evaluation of data reliability.
